text: ‘Bring a plea against’, literally, ‘approach’, but the context shows that the verb is used in a legal sense, as in 57.3 above and elsewhere.
The sense of the Hebrew text is, ‘guide thee’.

Literally, ‘he will fill thy soul with brightness and deliver thy bones’. But the Hebrew text means rather, ‘he will satisfy thy desire in time of drought, and refresh thy bones’, though the authority for this last verb is uncertain.
